paper_title: Experimental Studies on the Effects of Cadmium and Lead on Rats

methods.description: This method describes the experimental setup for studying the effects of cadmium and lead on rats, including the division of animals into groups, administration of substances, and collection of tissue samples for analysis.

steps.description: Experimental studies were carried out in the vivarium of the All-Russian Research Institute of Veterinary Sanitation, Hygiene and Ecology-branch of the FGBNU Federal Scientific Center -All-Russian Research Institute of Experimental Veterinary Medicine of the Russian Academy of Sciences on outbred white male rats weighing 215-230 g.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct animal experiments on white male rats

steps.description: The animals were divided into 3 groups: 1. the first group received cadmium nitrate and lead acetate (Reakhim) with food in doses, in terms of pure metal, 5.0 and 50.0 mg/kg (respectively); 2. the second group received food with cadmium and lead salts at the same doses and diatomaceous earth at a therapeutic dose of 5.0 g/kg of food; 3. the third group is the control, where the animals received the usual food (Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: divide animals into experimental and control groups and administer substances

steps.description: Two months later, the animals were sacrificed, and samples of the liver, heart, kidneys, spleen, testes, femur, and muscles were taken from the thigh.
steps.searchable_summary: collect tissue samples from sacrificed animals

methods.description: This method outlines the procedure for determining the concentration of cadmium and lead in tissue samples using atomic absorption spectroscopy.

steps.description: Atomic absorption determination was carried out under the following conditions: cadmium - resonant wavelength - 228.8 nm, spectral bandwidth of the monochromator - 0.5 nm, hollow cathode lamp, atomization temperature - 2100 °C; lead - resonant wavelength 217.0 nm, the spectral bandwidth of the monochromator - 0.4 nm, atomization temperature - 2200 °C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform atomic absorption spectroscopy for cadmium and lead

methods.description: This method describes the statistical analysis of the experimental results using the Student's method modified by Tippet in Excel 2010.

steps.description: Statistical processing of the results was carried out according to the Student's method modified by Tippet [16] in Excel 2010.
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis using Student's method in Excel
